briefdescription.para: The data.
The default constructor.

memberdef.argsstring: (const BoxArray &grids_, const DistributionMapping &dmap, int in_rad, int out_rad, int extent_rad, int ncomp)
memberdef.name: BndryRegisterT

briefdescription.para: The constructor, given number of cells in/out, extent and number of components (assumes cell-centered boxes, and allocates cell-centered FABs)
The destructor.

memberdef.argsstring: (const BoxArray &grids_, const DistributionMapping &dmap, int in_rad, int out_rad, int extent_rad, int ncomp)
memberdef.name: define

briefdescription.para: Allocate boundary FabSets for all faces.

parameterdescription.para: Number of layers inside each grid face.
Number of layers outside each grid face.

memberdef.argsstring: (Orientation face, IndexType typ, int in_rad, int out_rad, int extent_rad, int ncomp, const DistributionMapping &dm)
memberdef.name: define

briefdescription.para: Build FABs along a particular face orientation.
Release all FABs and clear the associated BoxArray.
Get box domain (as an array of boxes).
Return the number of grids in this domain.
Return const set of FABs bounding the domain grid boxes on orientation face.
Return set of FABs bounding the domain grid boxes on orientation face.
Set all boundary FABs to the scalar value v across every component.
Accumulate another register with identical layout into this one.
Convenience alias for operator+=.

memberdef.argsstring: (const MF &src, int nghost, int src_comp, int dest_comp, int num_comp, const Periodicity &period=Periodicity::NonPeriodic())
memberdef.name: copyFrom

briefdescription.para: Fill boundary FABs with num_comp components copied from src (component src_comp) into dest_comp, sampling nghost layers and honoring period.

memberdef.argsstring: (const MF &src, int nghost, int src_comp, int dest_comp, int num_comp, const Periodicity &period=Periodicity::NonPeriodic())
memberdef.name: plusFrom

briefdescription.para: Increment boundary FABs with num_comp components from src, using the same arguments as copyFrom().

memberdef.argsstring: (value_type a, const MF &mfa, int a_comp, value_type b, const MF &mfb, int b_comp, int dest_comp, int num_comp, int n_ghost=0)
memberdef.name: linComb

briefdescription.para: Linear combination: this := a * mfa + b * mfb on the boundary, using components a_comp/b_comp and writing to dest_comp for num_comp entries with n_ghost layers.
Set the BoxArray domain to grids once prior to calling define().

parameterdescription.para: Cell-centered grid layout that subsequent define() calls will honor.

briefdescription.para: Returns the DistributionMapping describing which MPI ranks own each boundary FAB.
Write all FabSets to disk (used for checkpoint data).

parameterdescription.para: Base name for the output file per orientation.
Output stream already opened by the caller.

briefdescription.para: Read all FabSets from disk (used for checkpoint data).

parameterdescription.para: Base name for the saved data.
Input stream describing the register metadata.

briefdescription.para: Local copy function for duplicating boundary registers.
A BndryRegister organizes FabSets bounding each grid in a BoxArray. A FabSet is maintained for each boundary orientation, as well as the BoxArray domain of definition.

detaileddescription.para: A BndryRegister object contains a list of FabSets bounding the grids in a BoxArray. The FabSet FABs are at the same refinement level as the grids they bound, and are accessed and modified via a variety of member functions.

Non-default instantiation allocates a set of FABs, grown into and out of the bounding surfaces of each box in the BoxArray. The width of the layer (in/out), as well as the "extent" of a bounding FABs (the number of nodes beyond the box boundaries, parallel to the grid surface) are determined by constructor argument. All boxes and FABs in this context are assumed to be cell-centered.

A small number of linear mathematical operations are provided for BndryRegisters, as well as overloaded [] operators for access based on grid boundary orientation. The BoxArray domain of definition is accessible, but not resettable,
